llm_response:
The team will commence the week with a collaborative brainstorming session to define the core requirements for the new app feature. Each developer must update their task tracking system with the initial design specifications before the end of the first day. This structured approach ensures that all members align on the project goals before diving into technical implementation.

### Monday: Planning and Design
The day begins with a morning stand-up meeting followed by two hours dedicated to architectural planning.
*   Review existing codebase documentation
*   Define API endpoints for the new feature
*   Create wireframes for user interfaces
*   Assign specific modules to team members

### Tuesday and Wednesday: Development Phase
These days are allocated primarily for writing clean and efficient code while adhering to established standards. Developers should refer to [Software Development Best Practices](https://www.atlassian.com/agile/software-development) for guidance on maintaining high-quality standards. The afternoon hours will be reserved for pair programming to resolve complex integration issues.

### Thursday: Testing and Quality Assurance
The focus shifts to rigorous testing to identify and rectify any bugs within the developed modules.
*   Execute unit tests for individual components
*   Perform integration testing across modules
*   Conduct user acceptance testing with stakeholders
*   Document all identified defects for the next sprint

### Friday: Review and Deployment Preparation
The week concludes with a comprehensive code review and preparation for the upcoming deployment cycle. The lead engineer will update the deployment pipeline configuration to reflect the latest changes. A final collaborative brainstorming session will address any remaining risks before the weekend. All team members must submit their final status reports by 5:00 PM.
